name: Ala Moana Beach Park
tagline: The locals' beach in the heart of Honolulu — Magic Island Lagoon is toddler paradise.
parking: Large free parking lot (fills weekends by 10am)
bestTime: Weekday mornings for space; sunset at Magic Island for golden light
talkStory: Ala Moana means 'path to the sea' in Hawaiian. Magic Island is actually a man-made peninsula built in the 1960s from coral fill — the lagoon on its east side is barely knee-deep, making it the best toddler swimming spot in Honolulu. Locals come at dawn to fish off the channel wall, and at dusk the peninsula tip has a front-row seat to Bowls, one of Oahu's heaviest reef breaks. Stake out a shaded pavilion before 8 AM on weekends or you're sitting in the sun.
